task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The session is configured for a Ganglion board in Synthetic mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI indicates 'Synthetic' mode and shows a 4-channel configuration (e.g., exactly 4 channels visible in the Time Series widget), confirming a Ganglion setup.

task_completion.description: The dashboard layout is divided into 4 panes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window area of the OpenBCI GUI is split into a 4-pane arrangement (typically a 2x2 grid).

task_completion.description: The Time Series, FFT Plot, Band Power, and EMG widgets are displayed in the 4 panes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown titles or headers of the 4 panes clearly show 'Time Series', 'FFT Plot', 'Band Power', and 'EMG'.

task_completion.description: The widgets are actively displaying live data for 4 channels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graphs in the widgets show active waveforms, frequency peaks, or power bars indicating data is flowing (not empty or static zero lines), specifically for 4 channels.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the dashboard is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command, file manager window, or GUI notification confirms that a file named 'ganglion_dashboard.png' has been saved in '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI interface is visible and interacted with, rather than just a terminal or text editor mocking the output.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply download or copy an existing image to the screenshot path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent actually configuring the GUI and taking the screenshot from within the GUI or using a screenshot tool on the active window.

integrity.description: The data shown in the widgets appears to be from the GUI's synthetic generator.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waveforms and plots look like typical OpenBCI synthetic data being rendered in real-time, not a static image loaded into an image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenBCI Ganglion board is a 4-channel bioamplifier. The required widgets are Time Series, FFT Plot, Band Power, and EMG. The session must be in Synthetic mode (generating 4 channels of mock data). The final screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/ganglion_dashboard.png.
